passes.title: Armenian Museum Of America
passes.benefit_text:
Boston by Foot pass admits 4 people on any regularly scheduled tour from May 1st through October 31st. No co-pay is charged with the pass.
Please reserve a Boston by Foot printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a timed-entry discounted admission ticket. You can enter the discount code into the discount code box on the Boston by Foot website (www.bostonbyfoot.org) when reserving tickets to tours in advance of the tour. First select the date you want to go by clicking "Calendar," then select the number of tickets. Once tickets are selected, enter the promo code found in your email in the discount code box. These passes are not valid on for annual events, Footloose on the Freedom Trail and Beacon with a Boo!
source_phrases: Boston by Foot pass admits 4 people on any regularly scheduled tour from May 1st through October 31st. No co-pay is charged with the pass.

Please reserve a Boston by Foot printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a timed-entry discounted admission ticket. You can enter the discount code into the discount code box on the Boston by Foot website (www.bostonbyfoot.org) when reserving tickets to tours in advance of the tour. First select the date you want to go by clicking "Calendar," then select the number of tickets. Once tickets are selected, enter the promo code found in your email in the discount code box. These passes are not valid on for annual events, Footloose on the Freedom Trail and Beacon with a Boo!

passes.title: Boston By Foot
passes.benefit_text:
Please note: The Boston Children's Museum hours change frequently. Please verify the date you wish to visit via their website before reserving your pass. 
Please reserve a Boston Children's Museum printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will need to show this pass, either printed or on your handheld device, for discounted entry to the museum.
After receiving the printable museum pass from the library, you then reserve your visit online with Boston Children’s Museum on their page here, under "Discount Tickets: https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing/. Advance tickets/reservations are highly recommended, especially during weekends, holidays and school vacation weeks.
The Boston Children's Museum pass admits 4 people. With the pass, the current co-pay is half price per person. The Museum is operating hours are from 9 AM to 4 PM.
source_phrases: Please note: The Boston Children's Museum hours change frequently. Please verify the date you wish to visit via their website before reserving your pass.

Please reserve a Boston Children's Museum printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will need to show this pass, either printed or on your handheld device, for discounted entry to the museum.

After receiving the printable museum pass from the library, you then reserve your visit online with Boston Children’s Museum on their page here, under "Discount Tickets: https://bostonchildrensmuseum.org/ticketing/. Advance tickets/reservations are highly recommended, especially during weekends, holidays and school vacation weeks.
The Boston Children's Museum pass admits 4 people. With the pass, the current co-pay is half price per person. The Museum is operating hours are from 9 AM to 4 PM.

passes.title: The Discovery Museums
passes.benefit_text:
Gore Place is pleased to announce the combined Mansion Tour, Take-a-Walk, and Story Time Library Pass for 2024-2025. Each pass permits two adults and two accompanied children to tour the 1806 Gore Mansion at the reduced rate of $5 per adult and free for the accompanied children--regularly $16 per adult and $11 for children ages 6-16. The pass also permits passholders to rent a Take-a-Walk bag for $2--regularly $5. We have expanded the program this year to also allow passholders to bring two children to a Wednesday Story Time-first Wednesday of each month, for $5 per child-regularly $10.
We are open for Mansion Tours, currently Mondays, Wednesdays, Fridays, and Saturdays. Days and times are subject to change, so it is best for visitors to consult our website at goreplace.org when planning their visit. Event and holiday closings as well as school vacation hours are all listed on our website.
Please note: The Mansion Tour pass is good for daytime Mansion tours only and may not be used during evening or special themed tours or during special events such as the Sheepshearing Festival at Gore Place.
source_phrases: Gore Place is pleased to announce the combined Mansion Tour, Take-a-Walk, and Story Time Library Pass for 2024-2025. Each pass permits two adults and two accompanied children to tour the 1806 Gore Mansion at the reduced rate of $5 per adult and free for the accompanied children--regularly $16 per adult and $11 for children ages 6-16. The pass also permits passholders to rent a Take-a-Walk bag for $2--regularly $5. We have expanded the program this year to also allow passholders to bring two children to a Wednesday Story Time-first Wednesday of each month, for $5 per child-regularly $10.

We are open for Mansion Tours, currently Mondays, Wednesdays, Fridays, and Saturdays. Days and times are subject to change, so it is best for visitors to consult our website at goreplace.org when planning their visit. Event and holiday closings as well as school vacation hours are all listed on our website.

Please note: The Mansion Tour pass is good for daytime Mansion tours only and may not be used during evening or special themed tours or during special events such as the Sheepshearing Festival at Gore Place.

passes.title: Harvard Museums Of Science And Culture
passes.benefit_text: Free admission for 2 adults and children under the age of 18 for 38 historic New England houses. Each home has different hours so please check their website at https://www.historicnewengland.org/ for the hours to each home.
source_phrases: Free admission for 2 adults and children under the age of 18 for 38 historic New England houses. Each home has different hours so please check their website at https://www.historicnewengland.org/ for the hours to each home.

passes.title: Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum
passes.benefit_text: Present your physical or digital Massachusetts library card at the Admissions Desk to receive a 50% discount on the cardholder's admission ticket. This offer is valid for visitors aged 13 and over and the visitor must present a Massachusetts library card to receive the discount. No pass is needed. Visitors aged 12 and under receive free admission. For hours and holidays, please reach out to the JFK Library Visitor Services Department with questions at kennedy.groupvisits@nara.gov or 617-514-1600.

passes.title: John F Kennedy Library And Museum
passes.benefit_text:
To make the admission process contactless for patrons, we are issuing--at Mass Audubon's directive--single use, one day passes printed out on sheets of library letterhead (instead of the usual laminated passes).  If you wish to visit sanctuaries that are using Mass Audubon's online ticketing system, you should request the pass several days in advance for the date you wish to visit, to increase the likelihood that tickets will still be available for that date.  Mass Audubon does not guarantee access on any specific date/time if tickets are not available.
The Mass Audubon Society pass admits up to four persons at one time (adults, seniors or children age two or older; children under two are admitted free) into any Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. The co-pay is $2 per person with the pass. The hours at Drumlin Farm are Tuesday-Sunday from 9 AM-5 PM. Other Audubon Society sites are open Tuesday-Sunday from sunrise to sunset.

The Mass Audubon Society pass admits up to four persons at one time (adults, seniors or children age two or older; children under two are admitted free) into any Mass Audubon wildlife sanctuary. The co-pay is $2 per person with the pass. The hours at Drumlin Farm are Tuesday-Sunday from 9 AM-5 PM. Other Audubon Society sites are open Tuesday-Sunday from sunrise to sunset.

passes.title: Massachusetts State Parks Department Of Conservation And Recreation
passes.benefit_text:
Starting on January 2, 2022, The Museum of Fine Arts is currently open six days a week:  Wednesday through Monday, 10 am–5 pm. As of January 2026, half price tickets will increase to $15.
Please reserve a Museum of Fine Arts printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers separated by a dash. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a discounted admission ticket through the Museum of Fine Arts website for admission to the museum. To reserve discounted tickets online, please go to mfa.org/tickets, select the date of your visit and enter the promo code in the top right corner of the next page, which will activate the reduced admission rate ticketing option of "Library Pass" and "Library Pass Youth."
Children 6 and under are admitted free. Each pass now allows admission for 2 adults at $10 each (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7–17 at $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for children 7–17 for general admission, currently $10. As of January 2026, half price tickets will increase to $15.
source_phrases: Starting on January 2, 2022, The Museum of Fine Arts is currently open six days a week:  Wednesday through Monday, 10 am–5 pm. As of January 2026, half price tickets will increase to $15.

Please reserve a Museum of Fine Arts printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Access Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers separated by a dash. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a discounted admission ticket through the Museum of Fine Arts website for admission to the museum. To reserve discounted tickets online, please go to mfa.org/tickets, select the date of your visit and enter the promo code in the top right corner of the next page, which will activate the reduced admission rate ticketing option of "Library Pass" and "Library Pass Youth."

Children 6 and under are admitted free. Each pass now allows admission for 2 adults at $10 each (general admission and special exhibition) and up to 4 children ages 7–17 at $10 each (special exhibition only). There is no discount for children 7–17 for general admission, currently $10. As of January 2026, half price tickets will increase to $15.

passes.title: Museum Of Fine Arts
passes.benefit_text:
This printable pass will admit up to 4 people at a 50% discount on tickets to the Museum’s Exhibit Halls. Children under 3 are not included in the count of 4 and are always admitted for free but must have a timed ticket.
 Your Library pass can only be used on the date printed on the pass. You no longer need to make a reservation. Just bring the print out pass with you.

passes.title: Museum Of Science
passes.benefit_text:
New England Aquarium pass admits 4 people with a 50% discount. The current admission rates can be found on the New England Aquarium's website here . Children under the age of 3 are always admitted free. The New England Aquarium is open on weekdays from 9 AM-5 PM and weekends from 9 AM-6 PM. The New England Aquarium opens at noon on New Year's Day and is closed on Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day.
Library pass holders no longer need to make reservations. Tickets cannot be pre-purchased online nor over the phone. The printable pass coupon that you receive via email must be presented at the Aquarium Box Office ("General Admission" line) at the time of the visit. Library pass holders will be granted immediate entry after purchasing discounted tickets, even if the Aquarium is sold out to the general public at the time.

The New England Aquarium opens at noon on New Year's Day and is closed on Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day.

The printable pass coupon that you receive via email must be presented at the Aquarium Box Office ("General Admission" line) at the time of the visit. Library pass holders will be granted immediate entry after purchasing discounted tickets, even if the Aquarium is sold out to the general public at the time.

passes.title: New England Aquarium
passes.benefit_text:
Each New England Botanic Garden Membership Card entitles the bearer to half-price admission for up to two library patrons. Admission information is available online at https://nebg.org/plan-your-visit/
Make sure you check with the museum if there is inclement weather .
Open daily: 10 a.m.–5 p.m.
Last admission: 4:30 p.m.
Please note: The Garden will be closed on January 1, November 27, and December 24, 25, & 31 for general daytime admission. For the safety of our guests, the Garden may close due to severe weather. Please check the website, Facebook and Instagram or call 508-869-6111 for updates.

passes.title: New England Botanic Garden At Tower Hill
Orchard House
passes.benefit_text:
Please reserve a Peabody Essex Museum printable museum pass through the Belmont Public Library; when you click "Print This Pass" in your confirmation email, the printable pass will open and you will see a promo code, located just beneath the barcode - it is a series of letters and numbers. You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a timed-entry discounted admission ticket through the Peabody Essex Museum website for admission to the museum. To reserve timed-entry discounted tickets online, please go to https://my.pem.org/events, select the date of your visit and enter the promo code in the top right corner of the next page, which will activate the reduced admission rate ticketing option of "Library Discount (limit 2)."
The Peabody Essex Museum pass admits 2 people for general museum admission. The co-pay is $12 per person. Youth who are 16 years old and younger receive free admission. The museum is open Mondays, Thursdays, Saturdays & Sundays 10 am–5 pm, Fridays 10 am-7 pm. The museum is closed on Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day.

You will use this code in its entirety to reserve a timed-entry discounted admission ticket through the Peabody Essex Museum website for admission to the museum. To reserve timed-entry discounted tickets online, please go to https://my.pem.org/events, select the date of your visit and enter the promo code in the top right corner of the next page, which will activate the reduced admission rate ticketing option of "Library Discount (limit 2)."

The Peabody Essex Museum pass admits 2 people for general museum admission. The co-pay is $12 per person. Youth who are 16 years old and younger receive free admission. The museum is open Mondays, Thursdays, Saturdays & Sundays 10 am–5 pm, Fridays 10 am-7 pm. The museum is closed on Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day.

passes.title: Peabody Essex Museum
passes.benefit_text: Patron must obtain a pass from the library and present it to the guest services staff upon arrival at the Museum to purchase a Heritage Pass, Combination Pass, or Plimoth Patuxet Only pass for up to six individuals at the reduced rate of $18 per adult; $12 per child ages 5-17.

passes.title: The Trustees Of The Reservations
passes.benefit_text:
Please pick up the pass at the Reference Desk in the Reference Room of the Belmont Public Library; open hours of service can be found on the library website homepage. You do not need to make an advanced timed ticket reservation either by phone or online in order to visit either of the Zoo New England locations.
The Zoo New England pass admits 6 people to the Franklin Park and Stone Zoos. The co-pay with the pass is $9 per adult and $6 per child. Children under 2 years old receive free admission. The Zoos are open Monday-Sunday from 10 AM-5 PM.
